Off Calendar Mar-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KAISER GYPSUM CO., INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Oren P. Noah of Brayton & Purcell LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and responding party; Erica J. Saxum of DeHay Elliston LLP (510.285.0748) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: granted as to plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages and granted as to plaintiff Sandra Leonard's loss of consortium cause of action. Defendants' objections to Mr. Crum's deposition testimony are overruled. Defendants' objections to Mr. Crosby's deposition testimony are overruled. As to loss of consortium, defendants satisfied their burden of production of making a prima facie case that Mrs. Leonard's cause of action is barred and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this cause of action. Since all of Mr. Leonard's claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. Leonard, Mrs. Leonard does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. (Zwicker v. Altamont Emergency Room Physicians Medical Group (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 26.) Plaintiffs' argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that "the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.")). Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of Aiken Welch, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kurt Putnam, Esq., of HASSARD BONNINGTON (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties John R. Leonard, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane, Inc.'s demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action (loss of consortium) and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first and second cause of action (negligence and strict liability). Since all of Mr. Leonard's claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. Leonard, Mrs. Leonard does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. Plaintiffs' argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that 'the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.')). Counsel for defendant is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
